title: Local Repo Development
description: Follow a local repository development workflow with git worktree setup, pre-commit code review, and sensitive information scanning.

input_schema.feature_name: string — short feature, fix, or task name used to identify the worktree and branch
input_schema.repo_path: string — local repository path, usually the current working directory
input_schema.commit_intent: string — optional summary of the changes that should be committed
input_schema.mr_url: string — optional merge request or pull request URL created for the work

instructions: Confirm the current directory is inside a git repository with git rev-parse --show-toplevel; if not, ask for the repository path.
Before editing, inspect git worktree list --porcelain and git rev-parse --git-dir to determine whether the current checkout is already a suitable linked worktree for the task.
If not in a suitable linked worktree, derive a kebab-case slug from the feature name and create a sibling worktree from origin/main, then origin/master, then local main/master, then HEAD.
Create a branch such as feature/<slug> or fix/<slug> and use a non-destructive unique path if the preferred branch or path already exists.
Never discard existing user changes; work around unrelated dirty files and ask only when they block the task.
Implement the requested change using the repository's existing build, style, and test conventions.
Before committing, perform a code review pass over git diff HEAD or the staged diff and fix material findings.
Before committing, scan local repository changes for sensitive information with gitleaks, detect-secrets, or a focused fallback scan; never print secret values.
Stage only intentional files and commit only after code review and sensitive information scan pass, or explicitly state any gate that could not be run.
After submitting a merge request or pull request, asynchronously track CI/CD until it passes, fails, or is canceled; inspect failing job logs and fix in-scope failures.

examples.input: Add a dashboard filter feature in /repo/app.
examples.output: Confirmed current checkout was not a linked worktree, created ../app-dashboard-filter from origin/main on branch feature/dashboard-filter, implemented the change, reviewed the diff, ran a secret scan, committed only the intended files, opened an MR, and asynchronously monitored CI/CD until the pipeline passed.
